title: phegopteris pennigera
intro: Phegopteris pennigera, also known as the ladder brake fern, is a species of plant belonging to the family Thelypteridaceae. It is native to tropical Africa and parts of Asia, and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The medicinal properties of Phegopteris pennigera include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Phegopteris pennigera is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, with the roots and leaves being used to make herbal teas and tonics that offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestion.
